findings.evidence: Def at line ~ `unweightedBasepointChangeClass ... := S.weightedBasepointChangeClass (fun _ => 1) hdeg (x₀ := x₀) (y₀ := y₀) rfl rfl`; weighted versions weightedBasepointChangeClass_swap/_add/_self carry identical proof bodies (`change S.divisorClass (pointDifference …) …; rw [← map_neg, ← pointDifference_swap]` etc.).
findings.fix: Prove the general (weighted) lemmas only and derive the unweighted ones, e.g. `unweightedBasepointChangeClass_swap hdeg x₀ y₀ := S.weightedBasepointChangeClass_swap (fun _ => 1) hdeg rfl rfl` (and likewise for _self/_add), rather than repeating the Subtype.ext/divisorClass proofs.
findings.issue: unweightedBasepointChangeClass_self, unweightedBasepointChangeClass_swap, and unweightedBasepointChangeClass_add re-derive their statements with independent `change`/`rw`/`simp` proofs, but unweightedBasepointChangeClass hdeg x₀ y₀ is by definition weightedBasepointChangeClass (fun _ => 1) hdeg rfl rfl, so each is the corresponding weighted lemma at w = fun _ => 1.

summary: The unweighted base-point-change lemmas (_self, _swap, _add) re-prove from scratch what their weighted analogues already establish in general, even though the unweighted class is definitionally the weighted class at constant weight 1. Derive the special cases from the general lemmas instead of duplicating the proofs.
